text: I trained a byte-level BPE tokenizer on TinyStories with maximum vocabulary size 10,000 and added `<|endoftext|>` as a special token. The run summary, vocabulary, and merges were serialized to `artifacts/tinystories/run.jsonl`, `artifacts/tinystories/vocab.jsonl`, and `artifacts/tinystories/merges.jsonl`; training took 18:23.29 wall-clock time and used 3459356 KB of peak resident memory (about 3.3 GiB), and the longest token is `b' accomplishment'` (15 bytes), which decodes to `' accomplishment'`. This makes sense because common English word pieces, especially words with a leading space, are frequent enough in TinyStories to become single BPE tokens.
The slowest part of my tokenizer training is the BPE merge loop: each merge iteration updates pair statistics for pre-tokens affected by the chosen merge.
